Update Center

Rules & paddle legality

Official rules, paddle approval, and ban-related updates that can affect tournament play.

All updatesNewsRules & paddle legalityPlayer news & trends

Two bodies decide legality

Approval by one body does not guarantee approval by the other — where you play decides which list to check.

USA Pickleball (USAP)

Amateur & sanctioned play

Governs Nationals, regional, and local sanctioned events. Tests dimensions, materials, surface grit (spin), and PBCoR — the rebound or “trampoline” power limit.

Live database: equipment.usapickleball.org

UPA-A

The pro tours (PPA & MLP)

Certifies equipment for the PPA Tour and MLP with independent, stricter lab testing for power, spin, and post-break-in changes.

List: upaa.unitedpickleball.com

Paddle legality watch

Recent removals, reviews, and re-approvals — scan the status, then jump to the source.

  • Facolos Pro Series Elite X Removed

    Failed surface-roughness (grit) limits; the production version differed from the model originally submitted for certification.

    USAPchecked 2026-03-24USA Pickleball Compliance
  • Proton Series Three Project Peacock (S3PE15) Under review

    Listed on USAP’s compliance page; the model stays on the approved list while the review is open.

    USAPchecked 2026-05-28USA Pickleball Compliance
  • Speedup Tide 14S Under review

    On USAP’s compliance page; remains approved while under review.

    USAPchecked 2026-05-27USA Pickleball Compliance
  • Six Zero Ruby Pro 14mm Under review

    An open compliance review on USAP’s list.

    USAPchecked 2025-11-20USA Pickleball Compliance
  • R.A.W. (Reign and Win) Multiple models Resolved

    Previously flagged, now marked resolved on USAP’s compliance page.

    USAPchecked 2026-03-11USA Pickleball Compliance
  • Luzz Pro list (several models) Under review

    Pulled from the UPA-A pro list in early 2025 over a submission mismatch; four models (Pro Blade 2, Pro-4 Inferno, Pro-Cannon, Tornazo) were back on the list by early 2026.

    UPA-Achecked 2025 → 2026The Dink
  • Selkirk Project Boomstik Elongated 16mm Note

    UPA-A approved the paddle without the MOI clamp accessories that ship with the retail version — the clamps are not part of the certified configuration.

    UPA-Achecked 202611 PicklesSee our review →

Paddle statuses were verified on 2026-06-26 from official and reputable sources and can change daily — always confirm on the official database before you compete. USAP (amateur) and UPA-A (pro) certify separately.

USAP ComplianceUSAP Approved DB

2026 rule changes

Effective January 1, 2026 — mostly clarifications that close gray areas, summarised in our own words.

Scoring

Rally scoring: a point is a point

In approved rally-scoring formats the game-winning point no longer has to be won on serve — either side can win the deciding rally, so big comebacks stay alive.

Serve

Volley serve must be “clear”

The word “clearly” was added to all three volley-serve requirements — clearly below the waist, paddle head clearly below the wrist, a clear upward arc — giving referees more room to fault borderline serves. A tennis-style toss-and-hit serve was rejected again.

Serve

Spin on the serve, clarified

You can spin the ball through paddle contact on the serve; you cannot pre-spin it with your hand before contact.

Faults

A visible spare ball is a fault

A spare ball that is visible during a live rally — even peeking from a pocket — is now a fault, to stop it from distracting opponents.

Line calls

Out calls must be prompt

An out call has to be made promptly — before the opponent strikes the ball or before it becomes dead — otherwise play continues.

Conduct

Stronger conduct enforcement

Referees can warn or issue a technical foul before the match (including warm-up), asking the crowd for help on a line call is penalized, and there is clearer authority to eject players for violence or venue damage.

Inclusion

Adaptive Standing Division formalized

A formal Adaptive Standing Division and clearer wheelchair rules were added — a wheelchair counts as part of the body, and eligible adaptive players may use a two-bounce allowance.

Check before you buy or compete

  1. 1
    Match the exact model name

    Thickness, shape, and version letters (14/16mm, elongated, Pro/Power) all have to match exactly.

  2. 2
    Search the right database

    USAP for amateur and sanctioned events; UPA-A for PPA and MLP pro divisions.

  3. 3
    Re-check right before the event

    Statuses can change daily and a removed paddle means forfeiture — screenshot the listing with the date.

Related update feed

Rules & paddle legalityJun 19, 2026

Approved paddle legality source

The USA Pickleball approved paddle list is the priority source for tournament legality checks. Approval, decertification, and model-name details should be verified at the original equipment list.

paddle approvalrulesequipment

Check before acting: Search the exact paddle model and approval status before tournament play.

Verify at source: USA Pickleball Approved Paddle List
Rules & paddle legalityJun 19, 2026

UPA-A/PPA pro rulebook source

For pro-tour rules affecting PPA Tour and Major League Pickleball style competition, use the UPA-A rulebook page as an official reference and check the exact clause before summarizing.

UPA-APPA rulesMLP rules

Check before acting: Verify the clause number and the event context before acting.

Verify at source: UPA-A Official Rulebook

Primary sources to verify

Reference source

USA Pickleball official rules

Checks: official rulebook changes · rule interpretations · rule PDFs

Source
Reference source

USA Pickleball approved paddle list

Checks: approved paddle list · decertified paddles · newly added paddles · tournament legality

Source
Reference source

UPA-A official rulebook

Checks: pro tour rules · PPA/MLP rule changes · equipment standards · scoring rules
